summary: Brudenell Social Club scores 24/100 for AEO (critical) and 31/100 for GEO (weak). The page lacks all structured data — the single highest-impact fix available. No FAQ content was found, leaving the page unable to surface in featured snippets or voice answers. Capacity is not stated, missing one of the most queried facts about a live music venue.
quick_wins: Add capacity to the page as a clearly labelled field (e.g. a <dl> or a dedicated 'Venue Facts' section with Capacity: X). This answers one of the highest-volume venue queries instantly.
Add a 'Getting Here' section with nearest tube/bus/train. Directly answers 'how to get to [venue]' queries in voice search and LLM responses.

priority_fixes: [CRITICAL] Implement MusicVenue JSON-LD schema with: name, url, address, telephone, description, maximumAttendeeCapacity, image, sameAs (Wikidata/Wikipedia/social). Estimated AEO impact: +12–16 points.

[HIGH] Build a FAQ section answering: 'What is the capacity?', 'How do I get there?', 'Is the venue accessible?', 'What is the age policy?', 'Is there parking nearby?'. Wrap in FAQPage schema. Estimated AEO impact: +10–14 points.
[HIGH] Add explicit capacity as a labelled data point in both visible text and schema (maximumAttendeeCapacity property). This answers the #1 factual venue query.
[MEDIUM] Add a 100–200 word venue history section: opening year, significant events, cultural significance. LLMs prize provenance data for citation confidence. Estimated GEO impact: +5–8 points.
[MEDIUM] Add an Accessibility section: step-free access, hearing loops, BSL, wheelchair spaces. Boosts both AEO (voice search) and GEO (topical completeness).
schema_recommendations: Implement MusicVenue schema (@type: MusicVenue) with all applicable properties: name, alternateName, url, address (PostalAddress), telephone, description, maximumAttendeeCapacity, image, openingHoursSpecification, sameAs, geo (GeoCoordinates).
Add Event schema for each listed event: name, startDate, location (MusicVenue), performer (MusicGroup), offers (Offer with price and availability), image.
Implement FAQPage schema with Question/Answer pairs for the top 5–8 venue queries. This is directly read by Google's Featured Snippets and People Also Ask.

Add sameAs array to venue entity linking to: Wikidata item, Wikipedia article (if exists), official social profiles (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter/X). This is the primary LLM entity disambiguation mechanism.
Add BreadcrumbList schema to reflect navigation hierarchy (e.g. Home > Venues > London > O2 Academy Brixton).
content_recommendations: Expand venue description to at least 400 words (currently ~160). Cover: venue history, atmosphere, programming genres, notable past acts, location advantages.
Add a 'About [Venue Name]' section covering: year opened, original purpose (if converted), significant performances, architectural/cultural heritage. Provides LLMs with citation-worthy provenance.

Create a FAQ section with: 'What is the capacity?', 'What are the nearest transport links?', 'Is the venue accessible for wheelchair users?', 'Can I bring my own food/drink?', 'What is the bag policy?', 'Is there an age restriction?'.
Add a dedicated Accessibility page or section. Include: step-free access routes, accessible toilets, hearing loop, BSL interpretation, companion ticket policy, viewing platforms.
Add a 'Getting Here' section with: nearest tube/rail stations and walking time, bus routes, parking options, cycling infrastructure. Format as bullet points for easy LLM extraction.

State the venue capacity prominently — ideally in a 'Venue Facts' summary box or as a labelled stat (Capacity: 4,921 standing / 2,300 seated). This data point is queried millions of times across voice, LLM, and featured snippet surfaces.
internal_linking_recommendations: Add contextual internal links from event pages to the venue info page (e.g. 'Venue information', 'Accessibility', 'Getting Here'). This builds crawl authority for venue-specific pages.
Increase internal link density. Add cross-links between: venue homepage, events listing, venue info, FAQ, accessibility, and directions pages.
Ensure each venue page links to its own canonical URL and uses consistent anchor text (e.g. always 'O2 Academy Brixton', never 'click here' or 'this venue').
Add a 'You may also like' or 'Other O2 venues' section linking to sister venues — this builds topical authority across the portfolio and helps LLMs understand the brand network.
